title: Health Signals
summary: Health signals follow medicine, prevention, public health, and the technology changing care delivery.

items.name: AI diagnostics are being tested more widely
items.summary: Clinical software is entering screening and triage workflows.

items.name: Mental health demand remains high
items.summary: Access and affordability continue to shape care.
items.whyItMatters: Mental health is a system issue as much as an individual one.

items.name: Telehealth platforms
items.summary: Remote consultation is a normal care pathway.
items.whyItMatters: The delivery model of healthcare is changing.

items.name: Wearable makers
items.summary: Consumer devices are collecting continuous health data.
items.whyItMatters: These devices influence self-management and prevention.

items.name: Health departments
items.summary: System planning and prevention live here.
items.whyItMatters: Policy determines care access and quality.

items.name: Atul Gawande
items.summary: Practical voice on medicine and systems.
items.whyItMatters: He makes health legible to general audiences.

items.name: Michael Marmot
items.whyItMatters: He explains why health is also a social question.

items.name: How much should health data be shared?
items.summary: Privacy and effectiveness pull in different directions.
items.whyItMatters: The data debate is now central to care design.

items.name: Is prevention underfunded?
items.summary: Many argue systems still reward treatment more than prevention.
items.whyItMatters: This affects long-term health costs and outcomes.
